text: Risk management is an important process for any project or business. It begins with identifying risks, meaning you think about what could go wrong. These might include budget problems, delays, or equipment failure. Once you know the risks, you create mitigation strategies to reduce their impact. For example, you might train staff to prevent mistakes. However, some risks still happen, so you also need contingency plans. These are backup actions, like having extra resources ready. Finally, risk management involves monitoring. You regularly check for new risks and review how well your strategies are working. This way, you can stay prepared and handle problems more effectively.
